title: S4+S6: closure extraction + cancel-task comment docs
description: (S4) Extract runBeforeHook/runPostToolHook closures to module-level functions in index.ts. (S6) Add SDK shape mismatch comments near each 'as unknown as' cast in cancel-task.ts. File: src/index.ts:894-915,1195-1210, src/tools/cancel-task.ts:336,414,428,480
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: S3+S5: shared formatters + shared hook types
description: Create src/hooks/_messages.ts for shared system-prompt formatter vocabulary, and src/hooks/types.ts for shared ToolExecuteInput/Output types to eliminate per-file duplication and as casts in index.ts. Files: new files + src/index.ts, all hooks
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: S1+S2: dead exports cleanup (ORCHESTRATOR_PROMPT, parseAndCorrect)
description: Remove deprecated ORCHESTRATOR_PROMPT export from orchestrator.ts and unused extractDelegationEnvelope/parseAndCorrect from delegation-envelope.ts. Files: src/agents/orchestrator.ts:305-306, src/config/delegation-envelope.ts:175-201, src/config/index.ts:6-8
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: W4: session/multiplexer ordering contract undocumented
description: GLOBAL_SESSION_AGENT_MAP must be populated before multiplexer onSessionCreated, but this ordering contract is undocumented. Fix: add comment. File: src/index.ts:829-845
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: W6: path-gating should fail closed on hook crash
description: Path-gating hook fails open (logs locally but never surfaces error to user or app.log). If it throws on a malformed write, the write is allowed through. Fix: fail closed for pathGating specifically, emit appLog error. File: src/index.ts:894-915, hooks/path-gating/hook.ts
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: W3+W5+S7: reviewer-enforcement improvements (counter, envelope parse, WorkAgent type)
description: Three reviewer-enforcement fixes: (W3) Add clarifying comment on council rejection count bump; (W5) Memoize envelope parsing per delegation to avoid 4-5x re-parse log spam; (S7) Derive WorkAgent type from WORK_AGENTS set to avoid dual source of truth. File: src/hooks/reviewer-enforcement/hook.ts
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: W2: path-gating ordering comment misleading
description: The comment claims path-gating 'runs first' but the actual contract is that subsequent hooks self-filter by tool name. Fix: tighten comment to describe actual behavior accurately. File: src/index.ts:890-994
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: W1: dead fallback in mergeAgentConfig
description: The ?? { ...pluginAgent, ...existing } fallback in mergeAgentConfig is unreachable since deepMerge only returns undefined when both args are undefined, but the call site always passes two objects. Fix: drop the fallback, use deepMerge's non-null result. File: src/index.ts:97-102
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: B2: orchestrator prompt sentinel guards against substring collision
description: The 'already injected' guard in experimental.chat.system.transform uses s.includes('<Role>') && s.includes('orchestrator') which can false-match or silently break. Fix: embed a unique sentinel comment in buildOrchestratorPrompt output and match on that. File: src/agents/orchestrator.ts, src/index.ts:1091-1115
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: B1: chat.message hook agent rewrite idempotency
description: The chat.message hook reassigns output.message.agent after resolveRuntimeAgentName but does not guard against re-assigning the same canonical name, and has no test asserting idempotency. Fix: only assign if agent !== output.message.agent. File: src/index.ts:1056-1076
close_reason: All oracle findings addressed. Full internal quality audit completed. See PR.

title: [DISCUSSION] Plan approval gate, builder task, glob support, reviewer enforcement
description: Four design decisions: 1) Plan approval gate - add awaiting_approval state machine? 2) Builder task:allow - keep or remove? 3) Path-gating glob support - implement minimatch or rename to prefixes? 4) Reviewer mandatory enforcement - add post-delegation hook or drop mandatory language?
close_reason: Decided: plan approval gate implemented with user-skip option; builder task:allow kept; prefix matching kept over glob; reviewer gate mandatory with escalation loop (2 rejections→council, council failure→user).

title: Add npx typosquat protection and rename-rendered URL test (M3, L5)
description: M3: Skills installer uses bare npx names that could be typosquatted. Add explicit package reference or pin. L5: Add test that flags any new alvinunreal references.
close_reason: Added npx typosquat hardening via package-verify.ts, --yes flag, and rename hygiene lint test. 1075 tests pass.

title: Add grill-with-docs rule, specialist timeouts, stream commands
description: Three medium-priority items from inspiration audit: 1) grill-with-docs invocation rule in orchestrator prompt for ambiguous requests. 2) Per-specialist timeouts (planner/researcher/reviewer 10min, builder 25min). 3) /new-stream /close-stream slash commands.
close_reason: Implemented all 3 features: grill-with-docs rule in orchestrator prompt, per-agent timeout config (10min/25min), /new-stream and /close-stream commands with state.md stream lifecycle. 1075 tests pass.

title: Add subtask_summary return protocol to specialist prompts
description: Missing return protocol from original design. Add structured <subtask_summary>{status, what_changed, files_touched, validation, risks}</subtask_summary> to all specialist prompts. Optionally add post-task validation hook.
close_reason: Added subtask_summary return protocol to builder, researcher, planner, reviewer, council prompts. Added orchestrator processing instructions.

title: Implement envelope enforcement with injection/denial (C2)
description: Currently envelope enforcement is logging-only after rzq softening. Add configurable enforcement: log | warn-inject | deny, with warn-inject as default. Surface extractDelegationEnvelopeV2.issues[] back to orchestrator for corrective guidance.
close_reason: Implemented: configurable log|warn-inject|deny enforcement modes with V2 parser issues surfaced. Default: warn-inject.

title: Fix PROJECT_STATE.md stale test counts and minor cleanup (M1, M7)
description: M1: Test count inconsistencies across PROJECT_STATE.md. M7: Verify councillor isnt spawned when council is disabled.
close_reason: Fixed: M1 test count inconsistencies resolved to 1058, L2 package.json description updated, L6 command name drift fixed

title: Fix orchestrator prompt rendering issues (M5, L4)
description: M5: Code-block soup in task exemplar has nested backticks rendering incorrectly. L4: Task exemplar self-contradictory - envelope shown outside prompt then says it goes inside. Clean up to show correct syntax.
close_reason: Fixed: M5 code-block soup cleaned up, L4 exemplar rendering now shows envelope inside prompt parameter

title: Fix path-gating denial signal and permission gaps (H2, H3, L1)
description: H2: Switch _denied -> is_denied in path-gating hook. H3: Add list/codesearch/webfetch to READ_TOOLS. L1: Fix applyDefaultPermissions call order asymmetry. Also add sentinel file check for audit.skills (L7).
close_reason: Fixed: H2 path-gating denial signal switched to is_denied, H3 READ_TOOLS extended, L1 symmetry fixed, M7 councillor gating verified

title: Fix metadata and code URLs (package.json, installer, error messages, brand asset)
description: Needs user input for correct upstream repo owner. Package.json repo/bugs/homepage URLs, cli/install.ts GITHUB_REPO, index.ts error message links, interview UI brand asset URL all point to wrong owners or outdated locations.
close_reason: Fixed package URLs, GITHUB_REPO, error messages, brand asset URL, and README attribution restored. Verified octini owner.

title: Fix remaining UI name references (TUI, auto-update, agent index, issue template, command drift)
description: Quick text replacements needed: TUI sidebar, auto-update notifications, agent index prefix, issue template config path, and command-name drift between setup-skills and setup-matt-pocock-skills
close_reason: Fixed: TUI sidebar, auto-update notifications, agent index prefix, issue template config path, command-name drift

title: Triage: Researcher notes write gap
description: Path-gating allows researcher to write .opencode/notes.md and .opencode/scratchpad.md, but SDK permissions deny write/edit - so path-gating case is unreachable. AGENTS.md says researcher 'may write observational notes/scratchpad context when allowed.' Fix: either add write:allow to researcher permissions or remove the path-gating case as dead code.
close_reason: Added RESEARCHER_TOOL_PERMISSIONS with write/edit. Path-gating researcher case is now reachable, allowing .opencode/notes.md and .opencode/scratchpad.md writes.

title: Cleanup: Reviewer write path-gating is dead code
description: Path-gating has case 'reviewer' that allows .md/.json/.yaml writes, but reviewer's SDK permissions deny write/edit so that path is unreachable. Either remove the case or implement the 'trivial fixes with explicit permission' workflow described in AGENTS.md.
close_reason: Removed reviewer write dead code from path-gating. Reviewer stays read-only at SDK level.

title: Consider: Read-budget reset behavior on task calls
description: The read-budget hook resets to zero on every task delegation. If orchestrator reads 4, delegates, reads 4, delegates - never trips threshold despite 8 total reads. Design question: should this track cumulative reads rather than consecutive? Low priority.
close_reason: Decision: keep consecutive-only tracking. Cumulative counters would not add value — read-budget is for catching runaway deep dives, not normal coordination reads between delegations.

title: Hardening: Default custom agents to constrained permissions
description: AGENT_TOOL_PERMISSIONS[agent.name] ?? { '*': 'allow' } gives custom agents full tool access by default. Change fallback to READ_BASH_TOOL_PERMISSIONS so custom agents get read + bash but need explicit opt-in for write tools.
close_reason: Custom agent fallback changed from '*' allow to READ_BASH_TOOL_PERMISSIONS. Custom agents now default to read + bash.

title: Hardening: Replace builder '*' allow with explicit allowlist
description: Builder has '*': 'allow' which grants full tool access. Replace with an explicit allowlist of tools builder actually needs (read, write, edit, apply_patch, bash, glob, grep, lsp, codesearch, ast_grep_*). Task/council_session/cancel_task remain denied.
close_reason: Replaced builder '*' allow with explicit allowlist (read/write/edit/apply_patch/bash/glob/grep/lsp/etc). Task/council remain denied.

title: Fix: Planner cannot write plan.md (functional bug)
description: Planner's SDK permissions deny write/edit but the design docs say it writes .opencode/plans/ artifacts. Path-gating has a planner case that's unreachable because SDK denies the tool first. Fix: add write:allow and edit:allow to planner's permissions in agents/index.ts.
close_reason: Added PLANNER_TOOL_PERMISSIONS with write/edit. Planner can now write plan.md artifacts.

title: Fix: apply_patch bypasses path-gating entirely
description: Path-gating WRITE_TOOLS only has write/edit. apply_patch tool can mutate files without inspection. Fix: add apply_patch to WRITE_TOOLS and teach extractFilePath to parse patch envelope target paths. Priority: HIGH.
close_reason: Added apply_patch to WRITE_TOOLS and extended extractFilePath to check args.file. Path-gating now catches apply_patch mutations.

title: Add git remote configuration guidance to startup init
description: After `git init`, the startup process should guide users to configure a git remote (e.g., GitHub repository). Add a prompt or follow-up step after /init or /init:all that asks if the user wants to add a remote, and if so, helps them configure it (e.g., by prompting for remote name and URL, or detecting common patterns like GitHub repos). This is especially important since the plugin will eventually be published/reused.
close_reason: Added git remote detection and post-init remote configuration guidance to startup process

title: Determine .opencode/ directory creation strategy for new projects
description: The .opencode/ directory contains project-specific context files like .opencode/plans/, .opencode/notes/, etc. Need to decide whether to create these directories eagerly during /init or /init:all, or lazily when an agent first tries to write to them. Lazy creation is preferred by the user if it works reliably. If lazy, ensure all specialist agents that write context files (planner, builder, etc.) create parent directories automatically before writing. If eager, add .opencode/ subdirectories to the startup init process.
close_reason: Research completed - no implementation needed.

comments.author: ryangking
comments.text:
Research completed. Lazy directory creation is already fully supported throughout the codebase:
1. `trans-genderian-orchestra/src/utils/context-files.ts` has `ensureDir()` using `mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })` before every `writeContextFile()` call
2. `trans-genderian-orchestra/src/hooks/context-orchestrator/hook.ts` also implements `ensureDir()` with `mkdir(..., { recursive: true })`
3. `trans-genderian-orchestra/src/hooks/image-hook.ts` uses `mkdirSync(..., { recursive: true })`
4. OpenCode's native `write` tool creates parent directories automatically

No eager creation during startup is needed. Agents writing to `.opencode/plans/`, `.opencode/notes/`, etc. will have parent directories created automatically on first write. Closing as resolved - no implementation needed.

title: Thorough review of specialist agent permissions
description: Perform a comprehensive audit of all agent permission maps (orchestrator, planner, researcher, builder, reviewer, council, councillor) to ensure each is empowered for its role but not over-empowered. Check: tool allowlists match role responsibilities, path gating doesn't inadvertently block legitimate work, no agent has unnecessary write/bash access. Reference the council's safeguard recommendations and the original design docs. Produce a report with recommended changes.
close_reason: Oracle review complete. Key findings: apply_patch bypasses path-gating (HIGH), planner cannot write plans (functional bug), builder '*' is too broad. New issues created for HIGH items.

title: Restrict or demote subtask tool for orchestrator
description: Already implemented: orchestrator permissions use `'*': 'deny'` which denies subtask by default. Prompt at src/agents/orchestrator.ts:178 explicitly prohibits generic subtasks. No further action needed unless we want to add `subtask: 'deny'` explicitly for clarity.

comments.author: ryangking
comments.text: Closed as already implemented: wildcard deny blocks subtask by default and the orchestrator prompt explicitly prohibits generic subtasks.

title: Add task() exemplar to orchestrator prompt
description: Modern models often need an in-context example to consistently emit correct tool calls. Add a complete, copy-pasteable `task()` delegation example to the orchestrator system prompt showing a successful envelope-embedded call with subagent_type, prompt, and background args. This should improve delegation consistency. File: src/agents/orchestrator.ts.
close_reason: Added concrete task() delegation exemplar to orchestrator prompt

title: Soften delegation envelope enforcement hook
description: Currently the delegation-envelope hook silently injects MISSING_ENVELOPE_REMINDER into prompts when extraction fails. This can balloon prompts and confuse models. Instead: log extraction failures explicitly (with the raw prompt snippet), and either fail loudly so the orchestrator retries cleanly, or apply a gentler nudge. File: src/hooks/delegation-envelope/hook.ts.
close_reason: Changed to log missing envelopes instead of silently injecting into prompts

title: Add per-session metrics logging for orchestrator reads vs delegations
description: Implement a counter per orchestrator session tracking {reads, greps, bashes, tasks}. Log the ratio at session close. If tasks / (reads+greps) is below ~0.3, flag as a leaky session. This helps verify whether safeguard changes (read-budget hook, bash removal) actually worked. Add to GLOBAL_SESSION_AGENT_MAP neighbor structure or a separate metrics store.
close_reason: Added per-session metrics logging on read-count reset in read-budget hook

title: Implement post-tool nudge for orchestrator reads
description: Extend the existing post-file-tool-nudge hook to trigger after any read/grep/glob call by the orchestrator, reminding it to delegate rather than investigate directly.
close_reason: Extended post-tool nudge to monitor glob/grep/ast_grep_search/lsp_diagnostics

title: Add orchestrator read-budget hook to prevent self-research
description: Implement a pre-tool hook that counts consecutive read/grep/glob calls without an intervening task delegation. After N=3, inject a warning. After N=5, hard-deny the read. This enforces the pure dispatcher mandate.
close_reason: Implemented read-budget hook: warning at 3, denial at 5 consecutive reads without delegation

title: Fix session tracking for child subagent sessions
description: GLOBAL_SESSION_AGENT_MAP is only populated in chat.message hook, missing background/child task sessions. Need to also map sessions on session.created events. Additionally, normalize subagent_type to lowercase before isAgentName validation to support custom display names. Verify with tests.
close_reason: Fixed: child sessions now get agent name registered in GLOBAL_SESSION_AGENT_MAP during session.created

title: Rename plugin from oh-my-opencode-slim to trans-genderian-orchestra
description: Rename the plugin package and all user-facing references from "oh-my-opencode-slim" / "omo-slim" to "trans-genderian-orchestra". This includes package.json, config file paths, documentation, and any hardcoded strings.
close_reason: Renamed plugin from oh-my-opencode-slim to trans-genderian-orchestra across all files, config paths, and docs. 0 remaining old-name references.

title: Adapt self-improvement skill for Dispatcher plugin
description: Port the oh-my-opencode-slim self-improvement/config skill to work with the Dispatcher plugin. Must respect orchestrator read-only constraint by delegating file writes to @builder. Rebrand paths from oh-my-opencode-slim to omo-dispatcher. Align agent names with Dispatcher roles (orchestrator, planner, researcher, builder, reviewer, council, councillor). Register in skill-loader.ts.
close_reason: Adapted self-improvement/config skill for Dispatcher: SKILL.md created, registered in skill-loader, tests pass.
